name: gitGraph
tagline: Visual diff analysis with dependency-aware risk scoring

coreConcept.primaryValueProp: PR/code review tool that shows change impact beyond text diffs — visualizes which files and features will break
coreConcept.targetAudience: Code reviewers, especially after AI-generated commits/PRs
coreConcept.keyInnovation: Dependency graph + diff overlay = risk-aware change visualization

supportedLanguages.importTracking: static imports + dynamic import() with string literal paths
static imports + require() with string literal paths + dynamic import()

supportedLanguages.parser: Custom AST parser (dart-style, no SDK in browser)

dynamicImport.rule: Only when path is string literal, skip computed/template paths

forces.folderGravity: files in same folder attract each other

defaultView.note: User can manually zoom/pan for large repos

edges.thickness: proportional to number of imports between files

interactions.click: expand file to show child nodes (functions/classes/variables)
interactions.ctrlCmdClick: jump to definition (VS Code: open file, Chrome: GitHub file view)
interactions.rightClick: context menu: details, mark as core path, copy path
interactions.hover: highlight all connected edges + show tooltip with file info
interactions.drag: reposition node (physics pauses for dragged node)

capabilities: filter: show only files importing X
filter: show only files in folder Y
filter: show only files with risk > threshold
search.ui: search bar pinned to top of graph view

corePathIndicator.style: glowing border + star badge on node

directlyAffected.label: red
directlyAffected.meaning: file appears in the diff (was modified)

indirectlyAffected.label: orange
indirectlyAffected.meaning: file imports a changed file (transitively) — may break

impactDirection.definition: Files that import changed files, transitively
impactDirection.rationale: Answers: 'what might break because of this change'
impactDirection.traversal: BFS from each changed file, following reverse import edges

fadeEffect.basis: BFS distance from nearest changed file

chromeExtension.lastResort: DOM scraping of PR files tab

vscodeExtension.prDetection: GitHub CLI (gh pr view) or manual URL

algorithm.secondary: in-degree count (how many files import this file)

display.redIntensity: darker red = higher risk for directly affected files
display.orangeIntensity: darker orange = higher risk for indirectly affected files
display.hoverDetail: tooltip shows risk context (importers count, centrality rank)

entryPoint.type: button injected on GitHub PR page
entryPoint.location: next to 'Files changed' tab or in PR header area
entryPoint.action: opens overlay/modal on current PR page

overlay.layout: graph takes 100% of modal, toolbar pinned to top

repoTree.optimization: Only fetch+parse files in dependency chain of changed files

deepScanToggle.description: Setting to parse ALL files vs only dependency chain

auth.privateRepos: GitHub personal access token (stored in chrome.storage.local)
auth.authenticatedPublic: optional token for 5000 req/hr rate limit

phases: 2. Fetch diff files list → render red nodes immediately
3. Fetch file tree → render all file nodes (green)
4. Parse imports for changed files → render orange nodes
6. Run risk scoring → apply color intensity

options: deep scan vs light scan toggle

sidebar.icon: custom graph icon in activity bar

editorTab.description: Pop graph into full editor tab for more space

prDetection.primary: git branch → GitHub CLI (gh pr view --json)

indexing.fileWatcher: watch for file changes, incremental re-index

structure.packages/core: Shared TypeScript core — graph logic, parser, diff analysis, risk scoring
structure.packages/chrome: Chrome extension — Manifest V3, imports core
structure.packages/vscode: VS Code extension — imports core directly

modules.parser: AST parsing for JS/TS/Dart → file/function/class/variable nodes
modules.graph-builder: Build adjacency list from parsed imports
modules.diff-analyzer: Take diff file list → classify nodes as red/orange/green
modules.risk-scorer: PageRank + in-degree → risk scores per node
modules.transitive-closure: BFS/DFS to find all downstream consumers

sharedCore.chromeUsage: bundled as ES modules (no WASM needed — pure TS)

graphRenderer.api: receives graph data → renders interactive canvas

architecture.note: Dropped WASM plan — pure TS core runs fine in both Chrome and VS Code webviews

excludePaths: array of glob patterns to exclude
corePaths: array of file paths marked as critical

parser: TS file with exports → correct nodes extracted
TS file with no exports → no nodes
JS file with module.exports → correct nodes
JS file with require() → correct edges
Dart file with public/private → correct filtering
Flutter widget file → widget classes detected
Dynamic import with string literal → edge created
Dynamic import with variable → edge skipped
Circular imports → handled without infinite loop
diffAnalyzer: Single file changed → correct red/green/orange classification
Core file changed → many orange nodes
Leaf file changed → only that file red, rest green
New file added → red node, no orange (nothing imports it yet)

coverage: Full pipeline: parse repo → build graph → apply diff → score risk
Real-world mini repo fixtures (5-10 files each)
Button appears on GitHub PR page
Click button → overlay opens with graph
Graph renders correct number of nodes
Jump to definition opens correct file
Graph layout consistency (same data → similar layout)
Color coding correct in diff mode
